purpose: Declares the PluginController singleton that owns plugin state and orchestrates everything: the FuncItem command array, options load/save, the preview window lifecycle, Scintilla viewport subclassing, two-way scroll sync between editor and preview, the debounced re-render timer, and the debounce-delay submenu. Notepad++ talks to the controller via PluginExports.cpp.
exports: PluginController::SetModule / Initialize / GetFunctions / Name / MessageProc / OnNotification / Shutdown
PluginController::TogglePreview / RefreshPreview / CopyRenderedHtml / ExportHtml / ExportPdf / PrintPreview / ShowAbout / ToggleAutoOpenMarkdown / ToggleMermaid / ToggleScrollSync

enum CommandIndex (ToggleCommand, RefreshCommand, CopyHtmlCommand, ExportHtmlCommand, ExportPdfCommand, PrintCommand, ToggleAutoOpenMarkdownCommand, ToggleScrollSyncCommand, ToggleMermaidCommand, AboutCommand, plus SeparatorOne-Four and CommandCount)
invariants: Strict singleton via Instance(); module_, nppData_, and options must be set (SetModule then Initialize) before any TogglePreview/Render call uses them.
The commands_ array is sized to CommandCount and its layout is what gets handed to Notepad++ via getFuncsArray - changing the enum order changes the menu order.
Scintilla subclass refData is reinterpret_cast'd from `this`; the controller must outlive the subclass (Shutdown removes it).
renderDebounceTimer_ is a SetTimer(nullptr,...) id that must be KillTimer'd on Shutdown and before re-arming.
gotchas: MessageProc currently always returns TRUE (consumes every WM_COMMAND) so it must not be reused for messages that need DefWindowProc-style handling.

Many bookkeeping fields exist purely to suppress sync feedback loops between editor and preview (suppressEditorToPreviewUntil_, suppressPreviewToEditorUntil_, lastEditor*, lastPreview*); maintain symmetry when touching scroll-sync code.
